findings.title: Hardcoded AWS Access Key ID
findings.message: A hardcoded AWS Access Key ID was detected. Anyone with access to this key can potentially make API calls to your AWS account. (Found: `AKIA************MPLE`)
findings.remediation: Remove the hardcoded secret from your code. Store secrets in environment variables or use a secret management service (like HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, or dotenv files configured in .gitignore).
findings.suggestedDiff:
<<<< CURRENT CODE
const AWS_ACCESS_KEY = "AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE";
==== SUGGESTED FIX
// Read from environment variable or configuration service
const secretValue = process.env.API_KEY || "";
>>>>

findings.title: Hardcoded Slack Webhook URL
findings.message: A Slack Incoming Webhook URL was detected. Attackers could spam your Slack channels or steal incoming integration data. (Found: `http*********************************************************************XXXX`)
findings.remediation: Remove the hardcoded secret from your code. Store secrets in environment variables or use a secret management service (like HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, or dotenv files configured in .gitignore).
findings.suggestedDiff:
<<<< CURRENT CODE
const slackWebhook = "https://hooks.slack.com/services/T00000000/B00000000/XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX";
==== SUGGESTED FIX
// Read from environment variable or configuration service
const secretValue = process.env.API_KEY || "";
>>>>

findings.title: Express Check Csurf Middleware Usage
findings.message: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.
findings.remediation: Implement sanitization/validation on input parameters. Restrict function permissions or update vulnerable implementation blocks.
findings.suggestedDiff:
<<<< CURRENT CODE
requires login
==== SUGGESTED FIX
// Review standard mitigation guidelines for javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.
// Sanitize input, restrict access controls, or apply configuration limits.
>>>>

findings.title: Detect Child Process
findings.message: Detected calls to child_process from a function argument `req`. This could lead to a command injection if the input is user controllable. Try to avoid calls to child_process, and if it is needed ensure user input is correctly sanitized or sandboxed.
findings.remediation: Implement sanitization/validation on input parameters. Restrict function permissions or update vulnerable implementation blocks.
findings.suggestedDiff:
<<<< CURRENT CODE
requires login
==== SUGGESTED FIX
// Review standard mitigation guidelines for javascript.lang.security.detect-child-process.detect-child-process.
// Sanitize input, restrict access controls, or apply configuration limits.
>>>>

findings.title: Md5 Used As Password
findings.message: It looks like MD5 is used as a password hash. MD5 is not considered a secure password hash because it can be cracked by an attacker in a short amount of time. Use a suitable password hashing function such as bcrypt. You can use the `bcrypt` node.js package.
findings.remediation: Implement sanitization/validation on input parameters. Restrict function permissions or update vulnerable implementation blocks.
findings.suggestedDiff:
<<<< CURRENT CODE
requires login
==== SUGGESTED FIX
// Review standard mitigation guidelines for javascript.lang.security.audit.md5-used-as-password.md5-used-as-password.
// Sanitize input, restrict access controls, or apply configuration limits.
>>>>

findings.title: Eval Detected
findings.message: Detected the use of eval(). eval() can be dangerous if used to evaluate dynamic content. If this content can be input from outside the program, this may be a code injection vulnerability. Ensure evaluated content is not definable by external sources.
findings.remediation: Implement sanitization/validation on input parameters. Restrict function permissions or update vulnerable implementation blocks.
findings.suggestedDiff:
<<<< CURRENT CODE
// Vulnerable: eval() execution of dynamic strings
eval(userInput);
==== SUGGESTED FIX
// Use JSON.parse or strict map lookups instead of dynamic code
const data = JSON.parse(userInput);
>>>>

findings.title: Code String Concat
findings.message: Found data from an Express or Next web request flowing to `eval`. If this data is user-controllable this can lead to execution of arbitrary system commands in the context of your application process. Avoid `eval` whenever possible.
findings.remediation: Implement sanitization/validation on input parameters. Restrict function permissions or update vulnerable implementation blocks.
findings.suggestedDiff:
<<<< CURRENT CODE
requires login
==== SUGGESTED FIX
// Review standard mitigation guidelines for javascript.lang.security.audit.code-string-concat.code-string-concat.
// Sanitize input, restrict access controls, or apply configuration limits.
>>>>

findings.title: Internal IP/Domain Leakage
findings.message: Internal IP address found hardcoded in source. While not a direct exploit, exposing internal network topologies aids attackers in mapping out lateral movement strategies within environments.
findings.remediation: Avoid hardcoding internal networks. Externalize connection parameters into dynamic environment configs.
findings.suggestedDiff:
<<<< CURRENT CODE
const dbPassword = "mysql://admin:SuperSecretPassword123@192.168.1.50:3306/prod_db";
==== SUGGESTED FIX
const internalServiceIp = process.env.INTERNAL_SERVICE_IP;
>>>>

findings.title: Insecure MD5/SHA-1 Hashing
findings.message: Use of weak/broken cryptographic hash algorithms (MD5 or SHA-1). These algorithms are vulnerable to collision attacks and should not be used for integrity checks or sensitive data hashing.
findings.remediation: Use secure modern hashing algorithms such as SHA-256 (e.g. `sha256`) or password-hashing schemes like bcrypt/argon2.
findings.suggestedDiff:
<<<< CURRENT CODE
const hash = crypto.createHash('md5').update(password).digest('hex');
==== SUGGESTED FIX
crypto.createHash('sha256');
>>>>

findings.title: Sensitive Data Logging
findings.message: Sensitive variables (like password, token, key) appear to be logged directly. This can leak credentials into log files, SIEM platforms, or console outputs.
findings.codeSnippet: console.log("User password hash: " + hash + " for password: " + password);
findings.remediation: Do not log sensitive variables. Redact or sanitize credentials before printing to console or log systems.
findings.suggestedDiff:
<<<< CURRENT CODE
console.log("User password hash: " + hash + " for password: " + password);
==== SUGGESTED FIX
// Redact sensitive details before logging
logger.info("Transaction processed for user " + userId);
>>>>

findings.title: Vulnerable Dependency: lodash
findings.message:
Manifest references lodash@4.17.15 which contains a known vulnerability (CVE-2021-23337).
Description: Prototype pollution in lodash allows remote attackers to inject properties onto Object.prototype via merge/zipObject/defaultsDeep functions.

findings.title: Vulnerable Dependency: express
findings.message:
Manifest references express@4.16.4 which contains a known vulnerability (CVE-2024-43796).
Description: Express open redirect vulnerability in redirect responses where paths are improperly parsed and lead to phishing/redirection attacks.

findings.title: Vulnerable Dependency: moment
findings.message:
Manifest references moment@2.29.1 which contains a known vulnerability (CVE-2022-31129).
Description: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) vulnerability in moment when parsing specific malformed date string formats.

findings.title: Vulnerable Dependency: jsonwebtoken
findings.message:
Manifest references jsonwebtoken@8.5.1 which contains a known vulnerability (CVE-2022-25883).
Description: Verification bypass vulnerability when verifying token signatures due to improper handling of key types.

findings.title: Vulnerable Dependency: requests
findings.message:
Manifest references requests@2.28.1 which contains a known vulnerability (CVE-2023-32681).
Description: Requests leaks Authorization headers on cross-origin redirects, potentially exposing API tokens to untrusted third-party sites.
findings.suggestedDiff:
<<<< CURRENT CODE
requests==2.28.1
==== SUGGESTED FIX
requests==2.31.0
>>>>
